threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus is late; real Hackney folk just shrug and check their phones. The Tube strikes are just background noise now.
Last night on the 133, a guy serenaded us with an off-key rendition of 'Wonderwall' while the driver just shook his head. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a street musician playing a saxophone while a couple danced in the rain outside the Neukölln Arcaden—pure Berlin moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you sip your coffee while dodging a spontaneous dance party in the street every Saturday morning. It's wild!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they can identify the exact BART delays just by the smell of burnt brakes. It’s an art, really.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy playing the sax on the bus. Everyone was annoyed at first, but by the time he finished, we all clapped. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the squares and street art, but it’s the hidden pasteleries with line-ups on Sunday mornings that truly define us.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewildered expression at the Sagrada Família's constant construction noise, while locals barely flinch.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' when you're waiting at Dalston Junction. That'll change fast, trust me.
Last night, a guy on the 69 was serenading the driver with an old Adele song. Only in Brixton would that be perfectly normal at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy serenading the rain outside Reuterplatz with an old guitar while the buses went by. Felt like I belonged.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, you'll find random street art pop-up galleries in abandoned buildings, showcasing local artists without any official permits. It's a wild mix of creativity.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know to never trust the BART schedule. If they aren’t checking their watch every 5 mins, they’re likely just passing through.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 near Union Square this morning. The bus driver stopped mid-route to help a lost tourist. You don’t see that kind of kindness everywhere.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals dine late, but many of us prefer a 9 PM dinner to beat the crowds—especially on weekends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their confusion over how to order a coffee. It's always 'un café amb llet, per favor,' with that endearing hesitation.
